questions.question: Explain the difference between Product Quality and Process Quality with examples.
keyPoints: Product Quality refers to characteristics of the final software product (functionality, reliability, usability)
Process Quality refers to how the software is developed (methods, tools, practices)
Example: Defect density is product metric, cycle time is process metric

questions.question: List and briefly explain the five levels of CMMI maturity model.
keyPoints: Level 1-Initial: Ad-hoc, unpredictable processes
Level 2-Repeatable: Basic project management processes established
Level 3-Defined: Processes are well-characterized and standardized
Level 4-Quantitatively Managed: Processes are measured and controlled
Level 5-Optimizing: Continuous process improvement using quantitative data

questions.question: What is Total Quality Management (TQM)? List its key principles.
keyPoints: TQM is a comprehensive approach to quality involving all employees
Key principles: Customer focus, continuous improvement, employee involvement, process approach, integrated system, strategic planning

questions.question: Explain the PDCA cycle and its importance in quality management.
keyPoints: PDCA stands for Plan, Do, Check, Act
Plan: Design improvement actions
Do: Implement changes on small scale
Check: Analyze results against expected outcomes
Act: Standardize successful changes or restart cycle
Provides continuous improvement framework

questions.question: What are the components of Cost of Quality? Explain each.
keyPoints: Prevention costs: Training, process improvement to prevent defects
Appraisal costs: Testing, inspection to detect defects
Internal failure costs: Defects found before customer delivery
External failure costs: Defects found by customers after delivery

questions.question: Differentiate between Quality Assurance and Quality Control.
keyPoints: QA is process-oriented, focuses on preventing defects through process improvement
QC is product-oriented, focuses on detecting defects through inspection and testing
QA happens during development, QC happens after
QA builds quality in, QC checks quality in

questions.question: What is Six Sigma? Explain the DMAIC methodology.
keyPoints: Six Sigma aims for 3.4 defects per million opportunities
DMAIC methodology: Define (problem), Measure (current performance), Analyze (root cause), Improve (implement solution), Control (sustain improvements)

questions.question: Discuss in detail the various software quality metrics used for measuring both product quality and process quality.
keyPoints: Product Quality Metrics: Defect density, Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF), Mean Time To Repair (MTTR), Availability, Usability measures, Performance metrics
Process Quality Metrics: Defect removal efficiency, Cycle time, Requirements stability, Test effectiveness, Process capability indices
Explain each metric with formula and interpretation
Discuss how these metrics help in improving quality

questions.question: Explain the ISO 9001 Quality Management System and CMMI frameworks. Compare their approaches to quality management.
keyPoints: ISO 9001: International standard for QMS, principles-based approach, certification focused
CMMI: Process improvement framework with maturity levels, capability levels
Comparison: ISO is universal, CMMI is software-specific; ISO is about quality system, CMMI is about process capability
Both aim for continuous improvement and customer satisfaction

questions.question: How does process maturity influence product quality? Discuss with reference to CMMI levels.
keyPoints: Higher maturity levels lead to more consistent product quality
Level 1 (Initial): Unpredictable processes, variable quality
Level 2 (Repeatable): Basic processes, some consistency
Level 3 (Defined): Standardized processes, predictable quality
Level 4 (Managed): Quantitative management, controlled quality
Level 5 (Optimizing): Continuous improvement, optimal quality
Examples of how each level affects defect rates, schedule predictability

questions.question: Describe the key product quality characteristics as per ISO/IEC 25010 standard.
keyPoints: Functional Suitability: Correctness, appropriateness
Performance Efficiency: Response time, resource utilization
Compatibility: Coexistence, interoperability
Usability: Learnability, operability, user error protection
Reliability: Maturity, fault tolerance, recoverability
Security: Confidentiality, integrity, non-repudiation
Maintainability: Analyzability, modifiability, testability
Portability: Adaptability, installability, replaceability
Explain each with examples
